meta_title: For Students: idea validation to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Idea Validation to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to validate your project ideas early to ensure technical feasibility using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Debugging frustration often stems from building overly complex features without clear requirements. By validating your idea first, you simplify the scope, making code easier to write and maintain. Use Claude Code to audit your logic and Antigravity to simulate user flows before you commit to heavy development cycles.
content.intro:
The screen is glowing, the cursor is blinking, and your terminal is bleeding red error logs. You’ve spent hours chasing a bug that feels like a ghost in the machine, only to realize the feature you're building is architecturally sound but fundamentally disconnected from what your users actually need. This is the 'Beginner’s Trap': grinding on code that doesn't solve a validated problem.

But what if you could kill the bugs before they are born? By shifting your focus from 'coding first' to 'validating first,' you turn your development process into a strategic mission. This guide will show you how to use modern AI tooling to validate your concepts, ensuring that every line of code you write serves a proven purpose, not just a technical headache.

steps.title: Define the Value Hypothesis
steps.description: Before touching the IDE, articulate exactly what problem you are solving. If you can't describe the solution in one sentence, your code will be just as confused.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Critique this project idea: [Insert Idea]. Identify the top three technical risks that would make this app impossible to debug.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project README and identify potential technical bottlenecks for a beginner.'

steps.title: Map the Minimum Viable Logic
steps.description: Break your app into the smallest possible functional units. This prevents 'feature creep' which is the primary source of untraceable bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a minimalist technical architecture diagram for [Idea]. Focus on the simplest possible data flow that satisfies the core user need.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a flowchart of the core user authentication and data storage logic.'

steps.title: Simulate Edge Cases
steps.description: Most debugging issues occur in the gaps between your code and external APIs. Anticipate these gaps before writing the logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: List 5 edge cases for this feature. How can I handle these gracefully in my code to prevent silent failures?
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'List all potential failure points for this API integration and suggest defensive coding patterns.'

steps.title: Automated Code Review
steps.description: Use AI to scan your skeleton code for logical inconsistencies before you build out the UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit this snippet for anti-patterns: [Insert Code]. Refactor for readability and error-handling clarity.

steps.title: User-Centric Feedback Loop
steps.description: Validate your prototype with a peer or potential user to ensure your 'fix' is actually a solution.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a 5-question user feedback survey based on this specific feature implementation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'How can I instrument this code to track user drop-offs during the core workflow?'

pro_tips: Always build for the 'happy path' first, but wrap every external API call in a try-catch block immediately.
If you find yourself writing more than 50 lines of code without testing, stop; you are building too fast and debugging will be impossible.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests before you write the actual implementation code (TDD lite).
If an error message makes no sense, paste the entire stack trace into Claude Code with the instruction 'explain this like a senior mentor'.
The most 'advanced' code is the code you don't have to write. If a feature feels too hard to debug, delete it and simplify the requirement.
content.next_steps: Once you have validated your logic and built your MVP, move into a 'hardened' phase by setting up CI/CD pipelines to catch bugs automatically as you push updates.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to transform your technical hurdles into a professional, validated product. Stop guessing—start engineering with confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
